title: Result - Estonia
content:
Welcome to Estonia, a Northern European gem with scenic peat bogs, Siberian-like forests, limestone-rich coastlines and over 1,500 islands boasting sandy beaches.
With a temperate climate influenced by the Baltic Sea, Estonia offers year-round charm. Explore majestic pine forests, hike ancient bogs, or celebrate the summer solstice with bonfires and barbecues. Stroll Tallinn’s Old Town, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, known for its Gothic spires, medieval charm, luxurious hotels and vibrant neighbourhoods.

## Unique food tradition
Kohuke (sweet, pressed curd snack): freshly pressed sweet cheese covered in chocolate or caramel.
99% of public services in Estonia are online, making it one of the world’s most advanced digital societies where digital signatures and technology are deeply embedded in daily life.

### Local legend or myth
Saarevaht is the Keeper of the Islands: a solitary soul who lives on an island and is very protective of his home and loved ones.

### Language of the Land
While Estonian is the national language, most Estonians, especially younger ones, speak English, making visitors feel right at home.
seasons occur, with the fifth being a natural flood that takes place between winter and spring.
